explanation: Without >>, code executes once and exits

description: HTTP server that handles multiple requests
explanation: Without .loop >> .loop, server exits after one request

description: TCP server that handles multiple connections
correct:
.sock: TCP
.bind: BND .sock .addr
.listen: LST .sock 10
.loop: ACC .sock
.buf: ALC 512
.n: RCV .loop .buf 512
.send: XMT .loop .buf .n
.cls: CLS .loop
.loop >> .loop
wrong:
.sock: TCP
.bind: BND .sock .addr
.listen: LST .sock 10
.client: ACC .sock
.buf: ALC 512
.n: RCV .client .buf 512
.send: XMT .client .buf .n
.cls: CLS .client
explanation: Without loop, server handles one connection and exits
PRT requires two arguments: buffer and length. Missing length causes silent failure.
Length must include all characters including \n. Count carefully or use SLN operator.

correct:
.caps: REQUIRES { http, json }
.server: HTTP.LISTEN 8080
.loop: HTTP.ACCEPT .server
.data: "{\"status\":\"ok\"}"
.resp: HTTP.RESPOND .loop 200 .data
.loop >> .loop
wrong:
.caps: REQUIRES { http, json }
.server: HTTP.LISTEN 8080
.req: HTTP.ACCEPT .server
.data: "{\"status\":\"ok\"}"
.resp: HTTP.RESPOND .req 200 .data
explanation: REST API without loop only handles one request
Not closing files can cause resource leaks
Not closing sockets causes resource leaks and port exhaustion

wrong:
.buf: ALC 1024
-- use buffer
-- forgot to free

explanation: Unclosed connections exhaust database connection pool
HTTP operations require REQUIRES { http } declaration

wrong:
x: 42
y: 10
sum: ADD x y
explanation: All identifiers must start with dot: .name
